questions.question: Structs are passed by Reference.
questions.explanation: False! Structs are Value Types. They are passed by cloning/copy.

questions.question: In a Dictionary, every Key must be exactly the same Type (e.g. all Strings).
questions.explanation: True. Swift collections are strongly typed.

questions.question: Looking up 'names[4]' on an Array with 3 items will return an Optional 'nil'.
questions.explanation: False. Arrays DO NOT return Optionals when lookups fail. They instantly crash the app (Index Out Of Bounds).

questions.question: Class methods require the 'mutating' keyword to change their own internal variables.
questions.explanation: False. Classes never need 'mutating'. Only Structs (Value Types) require that keyword for safety.

questions.question: A loop using 'continue' immediately exits completely.
questions.explanation: False. 'break' exits out entirely. 'continue' only skips the current iteration and loops again.

questions.question: The ?? coalescing operator provides a default value to use if an Optional is nil.
questions.explanation: True. It allows for ultra-fast, single-line fallback defaults.
